## Sanity
# Sanity MCP

The Sanity MCP Server provides tools to interact with Sanity content, schemas, and projects.

Common operations include:
- Querying documents using GROQ
- Creating and patching documents
- Fetching and updating schemas
- Creating and managing projects, organizations, and datasets
- Publishing changes and working with draft documents
- Creating and querying embeddings indexes for semantic search

## Best Practices

Before writing Sanity-related code, use `search_docs` and `read_docs` tools to review the Sanity documentation for changes since model training. Features and best practices may have been updated recently.
- Checking the available Sanity Rules with list_sanity_rules
  - For GROQ queries use the `groq` rule

  - Read the `get-started` rule for new projects

title:	Add Playwright e2e tests for Contact, Testimonials, Our Work, Post, and Category pages
state:	OPEN
author:	petercr
labels:	
comments:	0
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	47
--
## Summary

We have Playwright e2e tests for the homepage (`homepage.spec.ts`) and basic navigation (`navigation.spec.ts`), but no page-level tests for the other routes. This issue tracks adding full coverage.

## Pages to cover

### Static pages

**`e2e/contact.spec.ts`**
- Loads `/contact` and shows the `Contact` heading
- Displays all form fields: First Name, Last Name, Reason For Message, Additional Info
- Shows inline validation errors when required fields are left blank and form is submitted
- Clears errors as fields are filled in
- Disables the submit button while submitting
- Shows the success message after a successful submission (mock the `fetch` to `api.web3forms.com`)
- Shows an error message when the submission fails
- Shows `BackToHome` and `SocialLinks` components

**`e2e/testimonials.spec.ts`**
- Loads `/testimonials` and shows the `Testimonials` heading
- Renders at least one testimonial card (name + body text)
- Shows `BackToHome` and `SocialLinks` components

**`e2e/our-work.spec.ts`**
- Loads `/our-work` and shows the `Our Work` heading
- Renders at least one project card (project name + description visible)
- External project links open in a new tab (`target="_blank"`)
- Shows `BackToHome` and `SocialLinks` components

### Dynamic (CMS-driven) pages via the catch-all `/$` route

These rely on content existing in Sanity. Tests should either use a known seed slug or be skipped/conditionally run in CI when no content is available.

**`e2e/post.spec.ts`**
- Navigating to a known post slug renders the post title as an `<h1>`
- Author name, published date, and reading time are visible in the meta bar
- The post body content is rendered
- A `<script type="application/ld+json">` tag is present with valid JSON-LD
- Shows `BackToHome` and `SocialLinks` components
- 404 / not-found state: navigating to a non-existent slug shows the `NotFound` page

**`e2e/category.spec.ts`**
- Navigating to a known category slug renders the category title as an `<h1>`
- Category description/body text is visible
- If SEO keywords are present, the "Topics" section is rendered
- Shows `BackToHome` and `SocialLinks` components
- 404 / not-found state: navigating to a non-existent slug shows the `NotFound` page

## Notes

- Follow the existing pattern of importing from `./fixture` for `test` and `expect`
- The contact form's `fetch` call should be mocked with `page.route()` to avoid real network calls and to test both success and error branches
- Dynamic page tests should use `test.skip` with a descriptive message when running without a live Sanity dataset (check for an env var like `E2E_HAS_CONTENT`)
- Keep assertions focused on user-visible content — avoid testing implementation details or CSS class names

content.text:
Two changes made to `contact.spec.ts`:

1. **`waitForLoadState('networkidle')`** in `beforeEach` — waits until all JS bundles have finished loading and React has hydrated before any test interaction. This is the root cause: `page.goto` resolves at the browser `load` event, but React's hydration happens shortly after. Without waiting, clicking Submit triggers native form submission (page reload) instead of React's handler.

2. **`getByLabel('LinkedIn').first()`** — avoids the strict mode violation caused by two LinkedIn links on the page (one in `SocialLinks`, one in `Footer`).

The same LinkedIn fix was applied to `testimonials.spec.ts` and `our-work.spec.ts` preemptively.

content.text: The same 30s hang as before. The root cause is a deadlock: `await page.click()` is internally waiting for pending route handlers to settle, but the route handler is waiting for `release()` which is in the `finally` block that can't run until `click()` returns. The only clean escape is to move the mock entirely into the browser via `page.evaluate()`, which has no interaction with Playwright's route handler system.

content.text: The deadlock chain was: `await click()` → waits for route handler → route handler `await latch` → `release()` is after `click()` → nothing moves. By patching `window.fetch` inside the browser via `page.evaluate()`, the gate is entirely in the browser's JS event loop — Playwright's route system never sees it, so `click()` returns immediately and the button transitions to "Sending..." as expected.
